title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
SEO Insertions on Why Should Everyone Have at Least 2 Term Insurance Plans - Article Page
Keyword density and no. of target keywords has been mentioned as comments in the beginning
Suggested additional anchor texts are marked as comments in Yellow. (If required)
Body Content:
Keywords: can I buy two term insurance policies, term life insurance
A term plan is meant to safeguard your loved ones in the unfortunate event of your absence, making them a revered addition to any financial portfolio. After building up a corpus through regular and affordable premium payments, you can rest assured that your loved ones will be secure even when you aren’t around to give them a comfortable life. However, while with life insurance you have the security that an assured sum will be paid out to your loved ones even if you survive the policy’s maturity, the same may not always be the case with a term life insurance plan.
After all, in the latter case, your policy’s protection is only guaranteed for a specific ‘term.’ In other words, the survival benefits you receive from life insurance are not offered by a term insurance plan. This is where having a second term plan might come in handy. Keep reading to find out the benefits, restrictions, and process for claiming multiple insurance policies when you opt for two term insurance plans.
Benefits of getting two term insurance plans
If you’ve ever wondered, ‘can I buy two term insurance policies,’ the answer is yes. This strategy can help counteract some of the drawbacks of having just one term life insurance in your portfolio. Here are some of the benefits of two policies over one:
Extra security: You get double the protection when you subscribe to two term plans over one.
Death benefit: The beneficiary of the policies can avail of a death benefit from one or more policies.
Reduce chances of claim rejection: If one insurer were to reject your claim, there is a chance the other might not, ensuring the security of your nominee/beneficiary.
Helps you reach financial milestones faster: It is common for insurance plans to help you plan for the milestones in your life: from helping your child get married to supporting their education; each of these can be fulfilled with an insurance corpus that you can build up through one or more channels.
Death claim: The beneficiary will receive a lump sum payout if the policyholder dies before the policy term ends. This payout is known as ‘death claim.’ The payment will be made as per the terms and conditions of your term life insurance policy.
Restrictions
When you hold more than one insurance plan, there are certain restrictions that can apply. One of these is that the total sum assured from your insurance policies should not exceed the Human Life’s Value. The insurability a person has is based on a risk assessment conducted by the new insurer accounting for the policyholder's health, their annual income and their existing life cover from any prior policies.
Another customary thing to do is that a policyholder must detail their first insurance policy’s information to their second insurer. In case they opt for a third term life insurance plan, both the previous policy’s details should be represented accurately to their new insurer. In case the policyholder misrepresents any information regarding their prior policies, this can result in their claims being rejected.
Claiming multiple insurance policies
If you plan on claiming benefits from multiple insurance policies, all information should be furnished about your other policies to your insurance companies. As per guidelines laid down by the IRDAI, companies follow a standard protocol when policyholders are claiming multiple insurance plans at the same time. In case there are any issues with your claim, you can raise a complaint with your insurer which can be escalated to the IRDAI if you fail to receive a response.
How to apply for multiple term plans?
Approach an insurance agency to find policies from other companies.
You will likely need just one recent thorough medical examination that can be used as baseline medical information about you across all your insurers. This will save your time and money.
Clearly assess your insurance needs as they will change based on changes to your annual income and family responsibilities. Ensure that opting for more than one term life insurance is not a financial strain for you.
The earlier you buy your term plan(s), the lower the premium will be for you. As the premium amount will likely remain the same throughout your policy term, you might feel a lower financial strain to pay this amount when you buy your policy(ies) early.
Conclusion
Buying two term insurance policies might seem redundant on the surface but comes with many benefits such as reducing the strain of claims getting rejected, offering double the protection, and helping you reach your financial goals faster. Choose your policies wisely by going through reviews of the insurer by other policyholders and choosing companies with a good claim settlement ratio.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
full_pdf_text:
Why Everyone May Consider having at Least two Term Insurance Plans
A term plan is meant to safeguard your loved ones in the unfortunate event of your absence, making them an important addition to any financial portfolio. After building up a life cover corpus through regular premium payments, you can be rest assured that your loved ones will be financially secured even when you aren’t around to give them a comfortable life. However, while with life insurance you have the security that an assured sum will be paid out to your loved ones even if you survive the policy’s maturity, the same may not always be the case with a term life insurance plan.
Benefits of getting two term insurance plans
If you’ve ever wondered, ‘can I buy two term insurance policies,’ the answer is yes. Here are some of the benefits of two policies over one:
● Extra security: You get double the protection when you subscribe to two term plans over one. ● Death benefit: The nominee of the policies can avail of a death benefit from one or more policies. The nominee will receive a lump sum pay-out if the policyholder dies before the policy term ends. .’ The payment will be made as per the terms and conditions of your term life insurance policy. ● Reduce chances of claim rejection: If one insurer were to reject your claim for some reason, there is a chance the other might not, ensuring the security of your nominee. ● Helps you reach financial milestones faster even in your absence: It is common for insurance plans to help you plan for the milestones in your life: from helping your child get married to supporting their education in your absence.
Restrictions
When you hold more than one insurance plan, there are certain restrictions that can apply. One of these is that the total sum assured from your insurance policies should not exceed the Human Life’s Value. The insurability a person has is based on a risk assessment conducted by the new insurer accounting for the policyholder's health, their annual income, and their existing life cover from any prior policies.
Another customary thing to do is that a policyholder must detail their first insurance policy’s information to their second insurer. In case they opt for a third term life insurance plan, both the previous policy’s details should be represented accurately to their new insurer. In case the policyholder misrepresents any information regarding their prior policies, this can result in their claims rejection.
Claiming from multiple insurance policies
If your nominee plan on claiming benefits from multiple insurance policies, all information should be furnished about your other policies to your insurance companies. As per guidelines laid down by the Insurance Regulatory Development Authority of India (IRDAI), companies follow a standard protocol when policyholders are claiming benefit from multiple insurance plans at the same time. In case there
are any issues with your claim, your nominee can raise a complaint with your insurer which can be escalated to the IRDAI if you fail to receive a response.
Points to be considered while applying for multiple term plans
● Clearly assess your insurance needs as they will change based on changes to your annual income and family responsibilities. Ensure that opting for more than one term life insurance is not a financial strain for you. ● The earlier you buy your term plan(s), the lower the premium will be for you. As the premium amount will remain the same throughout your policy term, you might feel a lower financial strain to pay this amount when you buy your policy early.
Conclusion
Purchasing two separate term insurance policies may appear, at first glance, to be an unnecessary expenditure; however, doing so can provide numerous advantages, including alleviating the stress associated with claims being denied, increasing the amount of protection provided as you might need increased protection due to changing life goals as you age. Make an informed decision about the insurance you purchase by reading the evaluations of the insurer left by other policyholders and selecting insurance companies that have a favourable claim settlement ratio. While purchasing numerous term insurance policies to provide a larger coverage may be more costly than purchasing a single term insurance plan, they do come with several benefits. When the coverage is considerable, it's also a good idea to spread term insurance among many insurers. Furthermore, the permissible sum guaranteed amount may vary according on each company's underwriting rules.
Another reason to consider purchasing new term insurance is to pay off debt. After the original term plan purchase, you may have accumulated home, business, or other long-term debts. Depending only on initial term insurance may deprive dependents of future benefits if an extra term loan is not acquired that is like an unhedged house or business loan.
Disclaimer: The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
